assertion_results.text: The plan explicitly references reusing common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter for comma-separated multi-value query parameter parsing rather than writing new parsing logic (constraint 5.4)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md and reuse-analysis.md both explicitly reference calling apply_filter from common/src/db/query.rs. plan.md states 'Call apply_filter with the license string value. This function already handles parsing comma-separated values and generating the appropriate SQL IN clause. Do NOT write custom parsing or filtering logic.' reuse-analysis.md states 'No custom parsing or SQL generation code is written — apply_filter handles the full pipeline.'

assertion_results.text: The plan references following the filter pattern from modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs as a structural guide for implementing the license filter
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md states 'Follow the exact pattern used in modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs where the severity field is declared as an Option<String> in the advisory Query struct.' reuse-analysis.md: 'The advisory severity filter and the package license filter are structurally identical — both are optional string query parameters that filter a list endpoint using comma-separated values processed by apply_filter.'

assertion_results.text: The plan references using entity/src/package_license.rs for the JOIN query rather than writing raw SQL or creating a new entity
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md states 'The filter should target the license column via a JOIN to the package_license entity table (entity/src/package_license.rs). Use the existing SeaORM/entity relationship defined in package_license.rs to perform the join rather than writing raw SQL.' reuse-analysis.md confirms: 'the service JOINs through the package_license entity... no raw SQL is written and no new entity is created.'

assertion_results.text: The reuse analysis covers all 3 Reuse Candidates from the task description, explaining how each would be applied
assertion_results.evidence: reuse-analysis.md has dedicated sections for all 3: 'Reuse Candidate 1: common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter', 'Reuse Candidate 2: modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs', and 'Reuse Candidate 3: entity/src/package_license.rs'. Each includes what it provides, how it is reused, why reuse is appropriate, and where it is used.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections (constraint 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lists exactly: Modify list.rs and service/mod.rs; Create tests/api/package_license_filter.rs. The 'Scope Boundary' section states 'No other files are modified. The changes are strictly limited to the files listed in the task\'s Files to Modify and Files to Create sections.'

assertion_results.text: The plan does not propose creating new utility functions that duplicate the functionality of apply_filter (constraint 5.4)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Scope Boundary: 'No new utility functions are created — all filtering logic is delegated to the existing apply_filter function.' reuse-analysis.md 'What is NOT created' section: 'No new query parameter parsing utilities (would duplicate apply_filter).'
